evidence: MultiSeedExplorer.explore has a clearly named four-phase structure (fetch, identify, build, core) with each phase delegated to a focused private helper. The main method reads as a composition of collaborators.
CollocateProfileComparator.compareCollocateProfiles has a clean three-phase structure (buildRawAdjectiveProfiles, buildAdjectiveProfileList, sort) with no interleaved concerns.

ExploreResponseAssembler methods (formatSeedCollocates, formatDiscoveredNouns, formatCoreCollocates, formatAdjectiveProfile) are consistently extracted at the same granularity — balanced extraction with no micro-fragmentation.
LongIntHashMap is a careful open-addressing hash map with linear probing, sentinel values, and load-factor-triggered rehashing. The implementation is direct, correct, and well-commented.

ExplorationHandlers.handleSemanticFieldExplore unpacks ValidatedExploreRequest field-by-field into local variables (seed, resolvedConfig, relationType, topCollocates, minShared, minLogDice, nounsPerCollocate) before use. This adds 7 variable declarations before any logic. The record fields could be accessed inline or destructured into fewer named groups.

ExplorationHandlers.handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti calls parseBaseExploreRequest once for the nouns_per early-exit check, then validateExploreRequest which calls parseBaseExploreRequest again — redundant parsing of the query string on every request to this endpoint.

ExplorationHandlers.resolveRelationConfig sends a 400 HTTP response and returns null on error — mixing side effects (HTTP response) into a method that returns a value. Callers must do null-check-and-return, which creates repetitive control flow at three call sites.

SketchHandlers.executeRelationQueries uses BiFunction<RelationConfig, ExecutedSketch, Map<String,Object>> as a callback — the lambda in handleFullSketchForType is straightforward but the callback pattern for what is essentially a response-format switch adds indirection.

ConlluConverter.convertConlluToWplChunks has complex state management with 5 mutable variables (sentences, tokens, chunks, inSentence, sentencesInChunk) and a try-within-try-with-resources pattern. The comment explains the subtle error-path behavior. The logic is correct but dense.
Main.showUsage is a sequence of 30+ System.out.println calls — verbose but this is standard for CLI usage text.
SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern (the package-private overload with 5 parameters) is a moderately complex orchestration method but the logic is sequential and well-commented with phase markers.
CqlUtils.splitCqlTokens has good examples in its Javadoc and the implementation cleanly handles nested brackets and quoted strings.

strengths: MultiSeedExplorer and CollocateProfileComparator both use explicit, well-named phase decomposition that makes the algorithm readable at the orchestration level.
LongIntHashMap is a correctly implemented and well-documented specialized data structure.
ExploreResponseAssembler uses consistent extraction granularity with no micro-fragmentation or monolith methods.
CqlUtils demonstrates clean, well-tested string manipulation with balanced helper extraction.
low_level_elegance.issue_character: The main low-level issues are in ExplorationHandlers: verbose local-variable unpacking from a record, redundant query-string parsing, and a null-return-after-side-effect pattern that adds repetitive null-check boilerplate at multiple call sites.
low_level_elegance.score_rationale: Function and class internals are generally clean and intention-revealing across the codebase. ExplorationHandlers contains a cluster of low-level friction points that affect reading flow — none are bugs, but together they represent a quality gap compared to the cleaner orchestration in the exploration layer. The score reflects solid work with localized rough edges.

issues.summary: handleSemanticFieldExplore unpacks ValidatedExploreRequest into 7 separate local variables before any logic, inflating method length unnecessarily
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore: String seed = req.seedsRaw(); RelationConfig resolvedConfig = req.relationConfig(); String relationType = resolvedConfig.relationType()... int topCollocates = req.exploreParams().topCollocates(); int minShared = req.exploreParams().minShared(); double minLogDice = req.exploreParams().minLogDice(); int nounsPerCollocate = req.exploreParams().nounsPerCollocate(); — seven declarations before any logic.
The record fields and nested record fields could be accessed inline at each usage site, reducing visual noise in this relatively short method.
issues.suggestion: Access record fields inline at usage sites rather than unpacking them into local variables, or extract a single well-named local variable for the params sub-record if reuse justifies it. Alternatively, keep only the few fields that are used more than twice.

issues.summary: handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti parses the request URI query twice — once explicitly and once inside validateExploreRequest
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti: Map<String, String> rawParams = parseBaseExploreRequest(exchange); — parses query string.
Then: ValidatedExploreRequest req = validateExploreRequest(exchange); — which internally calls parseBaseExploreRequest(exchange) again.
The first parse is used only for the 'nouns_per' early-exit check. The result is discarded before the second parse.
issues.suggestion: Extract the nouns_per check into validateExploreRequest itself, or pass the already-parsed params map into validateExploreRequest to eliminate the redundant parse. The simplest fix is to check params.containsKey('nouns_per') after validateExploreRequest using req.params().

issues.summary: resolveRelationConfig sends a 400 HTTP response and returns null, forcing three callers to add identical null-check-and-return boilerplate
evidence: resolveRelationConfig: calls sendError(exchange, 400, ...) and returns null on two error paths.
handleSemanticFieldExplore: RelationConfig resolvedConfig = resolveRelationConfig(exchange, params); if (resolvedConfig == null) return;

handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti: RelationConfig resolvedConfig = req.relationConfig(); (uses validated result, but the validate-path also calls resolveRelationConfig with the same null-return check via validateExploreRequest which also checks null).
handleSemanticFieldExamples: RelationConfig resolvedConfig = resolveRelationConfig(exchange, params); if (resolvedConfig == null) return;
The null-check-and-return pattern appears three times as a direct consequence of the side-effecting null-return design.
issues.suggestion: Throw IllegalArgumentException from resolveRelationConfig instead of sending the response and returning null. The wrapWithErrorHandling wrapper will catch IAE and send the 400 response automatically. This eliminates the null checks in callers and makes the method side-effect-free.

root_causes: ExplorationHandlers accumulated complexity as new endpoints were added, with each endpoint replicating slightly different patterns rather than sharing a unified request-processing pipeline.
The null-return pattern predates the IAE-throw convention and was not refactored when wrapWithErrorHandling was introduced.
